variables.diff_failure_message: No alternate issue source/test diff found and no reusable open PR exists
variables.format_command:
CHANGED=$(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all | sed 's/^...//' | grep -E '[.](ts|tsx|js|jsx|json|md)$' | tr '
' ' ')

if [ -z "$CHANGED" ]; then
  echo 'No changed format-checked files'
else
  npx prettier --write $CHANGED >/tmp/luther-prettier-write.log 2>&1
  npx prettier --check $CHANGED 2>&1
fi
variables.lint_command:
CHANGED=$(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all | sed 's/^...//' | grep -E '[.](ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' | tr '
' ' ')
if [ -z "$CHANGED" ]; then
  echo 'No changed lint-checked files'
else
  npx eslint $CHANGED > "/tmp/luther-artifacts/llxprt-code-alt/lint-output.log" 2>&1
  status=$?
  tail -n 240 "/tmp/luther-artifacts/llxprt-code-alt/lint-output.log"
  exit $status
fi
variables.plan_static_content:
# Implementation Plan

1. Read {artifact_dir}/issue.md and confirm the requested behavior for issue #{issue_number}: preserve explicit tool call cancellation state when a stream emits a cancellation event.
2. Inspect packages/core/src/core/StreamProcessor.ts and the adjacent stream processor tests that cover cancellation handling.
3. Implement the smallest source change so cancellation state is retained until the next tool call begins.
4. Add or update focused regression tests proving cancelled and subsequent tool calls are reported separately.
5. Run targeted verification for the changed package/files only, then keep the final diff narrowly scoped to issue #{issue_number}; do not modify package lockfiles, generated notice files, .luther files, generated build output, or unrelated files.
